subject: Remembering George H. W. Bush
body:

| The White House • December 3, 2018
---
| | |
# **Remembering George H. W. Bush, ‘one of America’s greatest points of light’**

**On the day he turned 18** , just 6 months after the attack on Pearl Harbor, George H. W. Bush volunteered for combat duty in World War II. He would become the youngest aviator in U.S. naval history at the time, flying 58 combat missions—including one that saw his plane take enemy fire and require that he parachute into the Pacific Ocean to survive.

**That legacy alone** would be enough to earn our Nation’s eternal debt and gratitude. For George H. W. Bush, it was only the beginning of a lifetime of public service. He would go on to become a member of Congress, Ambassador to the United Nations, Director of Central Intelligence, Vice President, and, finally, President of the United States.

**“President Bush always found a way** to set the bar higher,” President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump said in a statement over the weekend. “It is with great sadness that we mark the passing of one of America’s greatest points of light.”

**President Trump has proclaimed** this Wednesday, December 5, as a National Day of Mourning as President Bush’s State Funeral is held in Washington.

[The President and First Lady’s statement on the death of George H. W. Bush](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=cde3948aef&e=3ee4985eea).

**‘A great American life’:** [President Trump’s official Proclamation](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=52fb75aef5&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **President Trump returns from the G20 Summit**

**Yesterday,** President Trump and the First Lady returned from Buenos Aires, Argentina, host of the 2018 G20 Summit. As world leaders gathered, President Trump kept his promise to put Americans first every step of the way.

**The summit kicked off** with a historic moment: the signing of the United States–Mexico–Canada Agreement (USMCA), which replaces the outdated NAFTA. For years, NAFTA helped incentivize offshoring, leading far too many manufacturing jobs to leave the United States. USMCA [will rebalance American trade for the 21st century](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d862d34327&e=3ee4985eea).

**Another crucial meeting** took place between President Trump and President Xi Jinping of China. Incredible progress was made on two fronts in particular. “President Xi, in a wonderful humanitarian gesture, has agreed to designate Fentanyl as a Controlled Substance, meaning that people selling Fentanyl to the United States will be subject to China’s maximum penalty under the law,” Press Secretary Sarah Sanders said in a statement.

**And on trade** , the two leaders struck an understanding that leaves “unlimited possibilities for both the United States and China,” President Trump said. In exchange for the Trump Administration agreeing not to raise tariffs on $200 billion worth of product to 25 percent at this time, China will agree to purchase a substantial amount of agricultural, energy, industrial, and other product from the United States to reduce the trade imbalance between America and China.

**Out****of respect for the Bush family** , President Trump has postponed a full press conference on these historic actions until after President Bush’s State Funeral.

[Watch: Highlights of President Trump at the G20 Summit in Argentina](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=304e443fdc&e=3ee4985eea)

name: Dana Bye
subject: Reflections on our journey
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
It's going to be a sprint until the polls close on November 6th, and I'm reaching out to ask for your help before one more important deadline.
| | Peter, What a journey it's been! As 2018 comes to a close, it's both inspiring and humbling to look back at what our campaign accomplished.

In less than two years, we were able to inspire and sustain a grassroots movement of supporters like you, flip the 7th, and bring accessible, accountable leadership back to the district.

But how did we get here?

As Abigail mentioned in her victory speech on Election Night, it all began with a conversation at a kitchen table, debating a long-shot bid for the House in a district considered "unwinnable" by a Democrat.

I joined the team as Campaign Manager soon after, and I quickly learned what voters across Central Virginia would find out: Abigail was the real deal.

And it didn't take long. Between Abigail's announcement and election day, we knocked on tens of thousands of doors, made hundreds of thousands of phone calls, and inspired nearly 6,000 volunteers to join our campaign.

Working together, we helped make history. We outraised our opponent and every other candidate in the history of the 7th District and we flipped a seat that had long been considered safely Republican.

And we did it all by talking about the issues that matter the most to the people of the 7th District - healthcare, jobs, the economy, education - these and other issues were the focus of our campaign, and we made it our mission to share our vision in every corner of the district.

And my friends, it worked.

We gave people something to vote for instead of focusing on what they should vote against. We listened, we shared, and we engaged. And as a result, we won.

But if we want to sustain the momentum of Abigail's historic victory, if we want to make sure she has the opportunity to build upon her work on behalf of the people of 7th District, then we have to start today. And that begins with a **[donation from you](https://act.myngp.com/el/mZoDeDzkBTBzbWwz7Wc09EtwaA00bm3LgR-ehumrIWE=/uIbfqVhyWrpo5iYq4a_jyxOsRB68NkMV0iOnpg1sqf8=?refcode=120418)** , the very people who made our success possible. [](https://act.myngp.com/el/mZoDeDzkBTBzbWwz7Wc09EtwaA00bm3LgR-ehumrIWE=/uIbfqVhyWrpo5iYq4a_jyxOsRB68NkMV0iOnpg1sqf8=?refcode=120418) It has been an honor to serve as Congresswoman-Elect Spanberger's Campaign Manager, and it has been a privilege to get to know all of you.

Thank you for your support, thank you for your commitment, and most of all, thank you for your continued belief in what we could accomplish together.

\- Dana _Spanberger for Congress Campaign Manager_ _Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat and the Congressmember-elect for Virginia 's 7th Congressional District. She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together. To learn more, please visit [AbigailSpanberger.com](https://act.myngp.com/el/mZoDeDzkBTBzbWwz7Wc09EtwaA00bm3LgR-ehumrIWE=/bNZ-uogtQr4_vxUnKJ-qbtWG3yMmy1EToEDEJ3AzSqI=)_

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/u_N4kwArmM0GA21qAK_Zfa8b2h0GXEmCSGQOMvNkhbE=/vXiFcsr7K5J5X5GpLqQ5q4T2-Ipb8f3lyqFkGhDTrhc=)
Dear Peter,
Over the last week, TNDP staff and I have traveled to West and Middle Tennessee for the first three stops on our 2018 ANALYZE • ORGANIZE • MOBILIZE Tour. Each stop includes a 4-part agenda: Welcome & Meeting Ground Rules (to ensure everyone has a chance to be heard!), TNDP & Election recap, Q&A and Open Discussion, and finally, Ideas for Moving Forward.

So far, we have discussed everything from candidate recruitment, to changes in county party outreach, to racial divisions within the party, in addition to gainful dialogue on progress made and lessons learned following the midterm elections.

Most importantly, these debrief sessions have served as a reminder of the resilient, forthright spirit of Tennessee Democrats. Check out the mid-way recap below and find an upcoming tour stop near you.

**We kicked off the tour last Thursday in West Tennessee with Democrats from Haywood to Henry County.**
**On Saturday, we welcomed a packed house of Democrats from Shelby and surrounding counties.**
**And last night, Democrats from Middle Tennessee joined us at our Murfreesboro stop.**

**Tonight (Tuesday, Dec. 4)** , we're meeting at the Plumbers and Pipefitters Local 752 Union Hall for the Nashville stop of the tour! Join us from **6-8pm CT** for a public forum -- an opportunity for you, as a member of the Tennessee Democratic Party, to discuss the 2018 election cycle and our path forward in 2019, 2020, and beyond.

**It 's free to attend, but please [click here to RSVP](https://act.myngp.com/el/u_N4kwArmM0GA21qAK_Zfa8b2h0GXEmCSGQOMvNkhbE=/XkZdcAb0O8kJh22KYa_9RPV9nytIBiNxkMnBNjvTcc0=) to let us know you're coming. **

Opportunities to [_**sponsor the tour**_](https://act.myngp.com/el/u_N4kwArmM0GA21qAK_Zfa8b2h0GXEmCSGQOMvNkhbE=/M4zluIre5qsJcjuCXcJCxxGhsvxLnFvUW2vgcYW7lfQ=) are also available to help defray costs. Sponsorship levels are detailed below.

And **[share the event on Facebook](https://act.myngp.com/el/u_N4kwArmM0GA21qAK_Zfa8b2h0GXEmCSGQOMvNkhbE=/XFNJHJNEzWFhS_XeUByzkl9JU5Vi3sKvOU9qoTlEU0U=)** to spread the word and invite friends, family, and neighbors.

Thank you so much for your support. We look forward to seeing you!

Mary and the TNDP Team
\--
Mary Mancini
Chair, Tennessee Democratic Party

subject: Vice President Pence: George H.W. Bush ‘Never Failed to Answer the Call to Serve His Country’
body:
As former President George H. W. Bush lay in state at the United States Capitol last night, Vice President Mike Pence offered condolences... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=31259e2780&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Vice President Pence: George H.W. Bush ‘Never Failed to Answer the Call to Serve His Country’**

As former President George H. W. Bush lay in state at the United States Capitol last night, Vice President Mike Pence offered condolences on behalf of the Nation to the Bush family and honored the 41st President’s extraordinary life.

"While he was known as the ‘quiet man,' it was not for lack of nerve or daring," the Vice President said of the 41st commander in chief, with former President George W. Bush and other Bush family members in attendance. “Today, on behalf of the First Family, and my family, and the American people, we offer our deepest sympathies and respects to your family. And we thank you for sharing this special man with our nation and the world.”

[Click here to continue reading.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6bba3227bc&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “Hours after the body of former President George H.W. Bush arrived at the U.S. Capitol on Monday, President Trump and first lady Melania Trump paid their respects to the 41st president of the United States. Trump visited the late president’s flag-draped casket in the building’s rotunda, where Bush will lie in state until Wednesday morning before being transported to the National Cathedral for a private state funeral, which Trump is scheduled to attend,” Nicole Darrah writes in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bb4839ce3f&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| “President Trump scored major successes at the G-20 summit that concluded over the weekend in Argentina. Specifically, the community of nations agreed in their official communique to ‘necessary reform’ of the World Trade Organization, a top White House priority,” financial expert Liz Peek writes in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=44d089d365&e=3ee4985eea). “In addition, the Chinese promised to up their purchases of U.S.- made goods and to discuss other demands in exchange for postponing an expected hike in tariffs.”
---
|
---
| “A majority of ‘non-citizens,’ including those with legal green card rights, are tapping into welfare programs set up to help poor and ailing Americans, a Census Bureau finding that bolsters President Trump’s concern about immigrants costing the nation,” Paul Bedard reports for the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f4fd780eb3&e=3ee4985eea)_. “In a new analysis of the latest numbers, from 2014, 63 percent of non-citizens are using a welfare program, and it grows to 70 percent for those here 10 years or more, confirming another concern that once immigrants tap into welfare, they don’t get off it.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3506ae6c68&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=011e6cfc37&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=77cb393df4)

name: Tim Ryan
subject: How have the General Motors layoffs affected you?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tim Ryan for Congress
body:
The GM layoffs will reverberate across the country. Tell us how they are affecting you >> | |
---
**Folks, I've been speaking out every way I possibly can about the General Motors layoffs that will hurt families in Ohio and across the country.** I've met with Vice President Mike Pence about a federal response. I will speak to GM CEO Mary Barra this week about building a different vehicle at the Lordstown plant, so workers can keep their jobs. I've called for Congressional hearings on how GM used the massive tax break they received last year. **But my most important mission is to make sure that GM, Congress, and the Trump Administration hear YOUR story. ** [Tell us how the GM layoffs are affecting you, your family, and your community. We will make sure your voice is heard >>](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90fd/5486deaa/24216b46/dcf6464/314893135/VEsH/ "https://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/s/submit-your-story-gm?utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=1+-+Tell+us+how+the+GM+layoffs+are+affecting&utm_campaign=gmstory&source=gmstory") [SUBMIT YOUR STORY](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90fd/5486deaa/24216b46/dcf6467/314893135/VEsE/ "https://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/s/submit-your-story-gm?utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=2+-+SUBMIT+YOUR+STORY&utm_campaign=gmstory&source=gmstory") _Disclaimer: We will only share your first name, city, and story. _ I remain heartbroken and furious about this situation, and the media has noticed: **WKBN:** _"Congressman Ryan has harsh words for GM after it pulls Chevy Cruze"_ **Washington Examiner:** "_Tim Ryan boils over in __floor_ _ speech after GM layoffs"_ **WKSU:** _"Rep. Tim Ryan Calls for Congressional Review of General Motors"_ GM Lordstown was an anchor for our community in Ohio's 13th District. I know across the country, these layoffs have effects that will echo far past one administration or one news cycle. I will fight tooth and nail to make sure the new Congress takes bold, immediate action to keep jobs in the United States. It's imperative that we generate more opportunities so workers and their families can earn a living wage, afford high-quality health care, send their kids to good schools, and retire with dignity. [Your voice deserves to be heard. If you're willing, please share your story about how the GM layoffs are affecting you and your community.](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90fd/5486deaa/24216b46/dcf6466/314893135/VEsF/ "http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/s/submit-your-story-gm?utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=3+-+Your+voice+deserves+to+be+heard+If+youre&utm_campaign=gmstory&source=gmstory") Thank you so much, \-- Tim P.S. If you don't have a story to share but know someone who might, [share our form on Facebook so that everyone's voice can be heard.](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90fd/5486deaa/24216b46/dcf6469/314893135/VEsC/ "https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Faction.timryanforcongress.com%2Fpage%2Fs%2Fsubmit-your-story-gm%3Fsource&subsource=fbshare&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=4+-+share+our+form+on+Facebook+so+that+every&utm_campaign=gmstory")

Tim Ryan is a champion for working families in Ohio's 13th District and is known for challenging both parties to do more to rebuild the middle class. Tim was elected to the U.S. House of Representatives in 2002 and has been fighting for working people ever since.

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: Tell Trump to support my jobs plan
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
|
---

| | | | President Trump, 2017: **"We never again will sacrifice Ohio jobs"**
---

| _Cleveland.com_ , last week: **"Youngstown area reels with news of GM closing Lordstown plant"**
---
Peter, those two quotes tell the story.
First Trump and his allies in Congress passed a tax bill that gave GM and other massive corporations huge tax breaks. And now instead of using that money to invest in Ohio, **GM decided to close their Lordstown plant.**
I have a plan to get rid of the incentives in the GOP tax bill that reward companies like GM for shipping jobs overseas. I'm challenging President Trump to support it. [**Will you join me?**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffda2/4462dc26/898b6629/201ef453/1879933202/VEsH/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e181204m")
[**Demand Trump support my plan to invest in American workers.**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffda2/4462dc26/898b6629/201ef453/1879933202/VEsE/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e181204m")
Workers in Lordstown don't need more rhetoric from their president. They need paychecks. They need to pay their mortgages and fill prescriptions and buy groceries.
**Ohio workers expect leaders in Washington to protect their jobs.** Trump and his allies should join me. And hey, if we make enough noise on social media, maybe Trump will take notice.
[**Let the other side know you support my plan and want them to do the same. Add your name >>**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffda2/4462dc26/898b6629/201ef453/1879933202/VEsF/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e181204m")

subject: Joe Manchin Must Not Lead the Senate Energy Committee

body:
We can't allow Senator Manchin to lead the Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee.

| [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7aca8/2f5ea88d/7aca5379/2cbda401/184144920/VEsH/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/manchin-senate-energy-committee?utm_source=ema_2018-12-04_manchin-petition&source=ema_2018-12-04_manchin-petition&utm_campaign=manchin-petition&utm_medium=ema") Friend, Senate Democrats must not allow Joe Manchin to become the ranking member of the Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee. I need your help to stop this. Our party must be wholly committed to ending America's dependence on fossil fuels and building a robust clean energy economy. And Joe Manchin literally shot climate legislation in one of his campaign ads. He supports Donald Trump's dirty energy agenda. He simply can't be trusted to make the bold, progressive decisions we need. I need your help in building a national outcry against this lack of leadership in Washington, D.C. on the most critical issue facing our planet -- climate change. Please, help me out right now by adding your name. [**Join me in calling on Senate Democrats to prove they are real climate warriors -- don't allow Senator Joe Manchin to lead the Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7aca8/2f5ea88d/7aca5379/2cbda401/184144920/VEsE/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/manchin-senate-energy-committee?utm_source=ema_2018-12-04_manchin-petition&source=ema_2018-12-04_manchin-petition&utm_campaign=manchin-petition&utm_medium=ema") Look, Joe Manchin has been a champion for affordable health care for every American. He's been a leader on issues you and I care deeply about. But on climate, he's simply wrong. Climate change is going to impact states like West Virginia profoundly. We know that investing in a clean energy future -- instead of maintaining a dependence on coal -- will create more jobs now and in the long run. It's time for progressives to band together and take a real, meaningful stand confronting climate change. We must be wholly committed to the work that lies ahead. But allowing Senator Joe Manchin to lead Democrats on the Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee would be the opposite of that. There's plenty of places Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer can find to use Joe Manchin as a leader, but the Energy Committee is the wrong place. **Climate warriors across the nation need to stand up right now and say:** [**Senator Manchin is the wrong choice for the Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7aca8/2f5ea88d/7aca5379/2cbda401/184144920/VEsF/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/manchin-senate-energy-committee?utm_source=ema_2018-12-04_manchin-petition&source=ema_2018-12-04_manchin-petition&utm_campaign=manchin-petition&utm_medium=ema") Together, we can push Democratic leadership in the right direction. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [SIGN ON »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7aca8/2f5ea88d/7aca5379/2cbda401/184144920/VEsC/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/manchin-senate-energy-committee?utm_source=ema_2018-12-04_manchin-petition&source=ema_2018-12-04_manchin-petition&utm_campaign=manchin-petition&utm_medium=ema")

body:
As America mourns the loss of its 41st President, George H. W. Bush, President Donald J. Trump and the First Lady have extended condolences... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=237b66ed3d&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • December 4, 2018
---
| | |
# **‘Thank you for sharing this special man with our Nation’**

**As America mourns the loss** of its 41st President, George H.W. Bush, President Donald J. Trump and the First Lady have extended condolences and appreciation to the entire Bush family on behalf of a grateful Nation.

**Last night** , President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump traveled to the U.S. Capitol to pay their respects as former President Bush lay in state. Earlier in the evening, Vice President Mike Pence paid tribute to the 41st commander-in-chief, remembering his character, wit, and lifelong commitment to public service.

**“On behalf of the First Family** , and my family, and the American people, we offer our deepest sympathies and respects to your family,” Vice President Pence told former President George W. Bush and other family members gathered in the Capitol Rotunda. “And we thank you for sharing this special man with our Nation and the world.”

**Today, First Lady Melania Trump** hosted former First Lady Laura Bush at the White House, giving her a personal tour of this year’s Christmas decorations and theme, “American treasures.” A few hours later, she and President Trump paid the Bush family a personal visit at Blair House across the street from the White House grounds.

**President Trump and the First** **Lady** will attend President Bush’s State Funeral ceremony tomorrow at 11 a.m. ET.

[Video: President Trump and the First Lady pay their respects.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=aeb68857b8&e=3ee4985eea)

**Read:** [The First Family’s statement on the death of George H. W. Bush](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=491a5c1681&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **Larry Kudlow: We expect China’s auto tariffs to go to ‘zero’**

**After signing the trade deal** that will replace NAFTA at the G20 Summit in Argentina last week, President Trump held breakthrough talks with Chinese President Xi Jinping that could reshape American trade for a generation.

**“President Xi and I want** this deal to happen,” President Trump tweeted today.

**As part of Saturday’s meeting** , China has agreed to start purchasing agricultural product from U.S. farmers immediately. Another huge point of concern for President Trump is Chinese auto tariffs on American cars, currently set at a staggering 40 percent. That topic was a key point of discussion during the two leaders’ working dinner.

**Larry Kudlow,** Director of the National Economic Council, said that while a specific deal would need to be negotiated, Presidents Trump and Xi reached an “understanding” in Argentina that China’s 40 percent tariffs would soon disappear. “We expect those tariffs to go to zero,” Kudlow said—adding that America will know soon whether China is serious about an agreement.

[President Trump championed growth and prosperity at the G20 Summit](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4d749dd133&e=3ee4985eea).
